key_departures.impact: Starter-caliber WR released at start of new league year (post-June 1 designation). Missed all of 2025 rehabbing knee injury. Contract guarantees voided after he went AWOL during rehab. Creates significant WR depth hole alongside Jennings departure.

Team's leading receiver in 2025 with 55 catches, 643 yards, 9 TDs. Priced himself out of SF range, estimated market value ~$22.6M/yr per Spotrac. The 49ers chose not to match anticipated asking price. With Aiyuk also gone, SF loses both top WRs.
Starting left guard for four seasons in SF. Signed 4yr/$77M deal with Packers. Creates a genuine starting hole at left guard with no direct replacement on roster.

Retired one day before a $1M roster bonus and before the team had to decide on a $14.635M option bonus. Had 4 sacks in 2025 but zero sacks in final 10 games. Retirement opens a bigger hole in an already-weak pass rush unit that finished last in the NFL with 20 sacks.

Starting DT for 2 seasons in SF, started 16 games in 2025. Left for 2yr/$8M deal with Titans. Was part of an interior DL that produced just 1.5 sacks from the DT position in 2025. His departure escalates the IDL need but Odighizuwa trade directly addresses it.
Veteran rotational WR signed 2yr/$12M deal with Cardinals. Third WR off the board from SF's WR room in this cycle. Deepens the WR need but was not a starter.
Primary return specialist in 2025 (27.5 yds/KR avg, 11.6 yds/PR avg). Signed 1yr/$4.5M deal with Packers. Loss of elite return specialist creates a special teams need.
49ers declined to tender him as an RFA, letting him walk. Rotational/depth EDGE. Modest impact given Huff also retiring.
Backup center/IOL departed to Cowboys. Toth signing signals he was the replacement anyway. Low individual impact.

contract.notes: Base value $42.4M with incentives up to $60.4M per Adam Schefter/ESPN. $16.3M guaranteed per Ari Meirov/Spotrac. Incentive-heavy structure limits downside risk.

signings.analysis: Evans is the clear WR1 and steps in as the 'X' receiver immediately, giving Purdy a proven downfield threat and red-zone weapon. The 3-year structure with only $16.3M guaranteed and heavy incentive reliance signals the team expects this to function as 1-2 real years of commitment. With Aiyuk and Jennings both gone, Evans partially addresses WR but does not fully close the need — the room behind him (Pearsall, Robinson, Cowing) lacks proven starters. WR remains a priority in the draft.

contract.notes: Trade acquisition — SF sent 2026 3rd-round pick (No. 92 overall) to DAL. Odighizuwa under contract through 2028: $16.25M fully guaranteed in 2026, $20M in 2027, $20M in 2028. Originally signed 4yr/$80M extension with DAL in March 2025 with $52M total guarantees per OTC/Spotrac.

signings.analysis: Massive upgrade at a position that produced just 1.5 sacks from the DT spot in 2025 — the fewest in the NFL. Odighizuwa ranked 1st among DTs in QB hits (23) and 5th in pass rush win rate in 2025. His 3-year contract at $18.75M AAV is mid-to-upper starter tier for interior DL. With $16.25M fully guaranteed in Year 1 and $20M base salaries each of the following two seasons, this is a real multi-year commitment. IDL need is fully addressed. The cost of a 3rd-round pick is significant and reduces their draft capital at pick No. 92.

contract.notes: One-year deal worth $7.5M per Ian Rapoport/NFL Network and multiple reports. Broncos released him as a post-June 1 designation.

signings.analysis: Greenlaw's reunion is welcome but the one-year structure is essentially a prove-it deal given his extensive injury history (torn Achilles in SB LVIII, quad/hamstring limiting him to 8 games in Denver in 2025). He slots back next to Fred Warner (returning from fractured ankle) as the starting ILB pair. At $7.5M AAV for LB that is solidly starter-tier. Partially addresses LB for 2026 but zero long-term guarantee means the need persists beyond this year.

contract.notes: Four-year extension per Ian Rapoport/NFL Network and agents Drew Rosenhaus and Robert Bailey. $10M guaranteed.

signings.analysis: Piñeiro went 28-of-29 on FG attempts in 2025, leading the NFL in FG% at 96.6%. Re-signed before he could hit the open market. Four years with $10M guaranteed on a kicker at his level is fair value — Spotrac estimated his market at $21M AAV. Locks up the specialist position the 49ers desperately needed to stabilize after years of Jake Moody struggles. K need completely resolved for the foreseeable future.

contract.notes: 2yr/$12M with $5.75M guaranteed per ESPN's Jeremy Fowler.

signings.analysis: Lowe has 25 career starts in 4 NFL seasons with NE. He projects as a swing tackle in SF — potential LT1 if Trent Williams situation deteriorates, more likely as backup behind McKivitz. At $6M AAV this is depth/swing pricing. Does NOT resolve the Trent Williams uncertainty. OT remains a need, especially if Williams departs. Two years/$12M with only $5.75M guaranteed suggests limited commitment to him as a starter.

contract.notes: 2yr/$8M with $5.5M guaranteed per Tom Pelissero/NFL Network.

signings.analysis: Tonges is the bridge TE while Kittle (torn Achilles in January 2026) recovers. He stepped up with 34 catches, 293 yards, 5 TDs in 2025 filling in for Kittle. Re-signing him for 2 years with reasonable guarantees is the right move. He is NOT a Kittle-tier starter but is functional. TE need is partially addressed — when Kittle returns, this position is fine, but early 2026 games without Kittle are a concern. Good depth/insurance value.

contract.notes: One-year deal per Dave Zangaro/NBC Sports Philadelphia and ESPN's Jeremy Fowler. 95% of deal guaranteed per Fowler. Exact figures not reported; estimate based on IOL depth market.

signings.analysis: Toth is a versatile backup who played LG and C for Eagles in 2025 (4 starts in 17 games). He replaces Matt Hennessy as the primary interior backup and can compete for the starting LG role, but his 6 career starts makes him a backup solution at best. The left guard starter vacancy (created by Banks departing) remains a genuine draft/free agency need. IOL minimally addressed — Toth adds depth but does not solve the starting LG hole.

contract.notes: 1-year deal worth up to $4.5M per NFL Network's Tom Pelissero. Base $4M per Aaron Wilson. Packers cut Hobbs after giving him 4yr/$48M just one year ago.

signings.analysis: Hobbs is a proven slot/CB who battled knee injuries limiting him to 11 games with Green Bay in 2025. PFF rated him among the best run-defending corners in football when healthy. At $4M base on a 1-year deal, this is a low-risk reclamation play. He competes with Lenoir, Green, and Stout for CB spots. SF's CB room (Lenoir, Green retained) is not dramatically thin, so this is quality depth, not a need-filler. CB need minimally addressed.

contract.notes: One-year extension per team announcement. Exact value not publicly reported; estimate based on LS market.

signings.analysis: Veteran Pro Bowl long snapper re-signed to keep the special teams operation intact alongside Piñeiro. Continuity signing. LS position locked up.

recommended_need_adjustments.reasoning: Evans signing partially addressed WR but with Aiyuk (released), Jennings (departed), Bourne (Cardinals), and Moore (Packers) all gone, SF lost 4 WRs this cycle. Evans is WR1 but the room behind him — Ricky Pearsall (injury-prone), Demarcus Robinson (slot/depth), Jacob Cowing, Jordan Watkins — is extremely thin. WR remains the top remaining need. Draft WR early and potentially still active in FA market.

Pre-FA top need and it got WORSE. Bryce Huff retired (was the team's top edge rusher with 4 sacks), Robert Beal Jr. left, and no EDGE was signed. Nick Bosa returns from torn ACL (missed all or most of 2025) but his return timeline and health are uncertain. The 49ers finished last in the NFL with 20 sacks in 2025. EDGE is a critical tier-1 need heading into the draft.

Odighizuwa trade fully addresses this. He ranked 1st in QB hits among DTs in 2025 (23) and his 3-year/$56.25M contract (under the original $80M/4yr deal structure) is a real commitment. With Odighizuwa in hand and Alfred Collins/Mykel Williams on the roster, IDL drops to a tier-3 developmental need at most.

Aaron Banks departure (4yr/$77M to Packers) leaves a genuine starting LG vacancy. Toth (backup) and the existing internal competition (Nick Zakelj, Connor Colby) do not present starter-quality solutions. The Trent Williams situation also clouds the OT picture. IOL — specifically left guard — escalates to a tier-1 need heading into the draft.

Trent Williams situation remains unresolved. He carries a $38.84M cap hit in 2026 and the two sides have been at an impasse on a restructure. Lowe's signing provides swing-tackle depth but does NOT address a potential Williams departure. If Williams is traded or released (unlikely given $34M dead cap), OT becomes tier-1. As-is, OT stays tier-2 given Williams is expected to remain despite the uncertainty.

Greenlaw re-signing (1yr/$7.5M) + Fred Warner's return from ankle injury gives the 49ers a legitimate starting pair. Dee Winters, Tatum Bethune, and Nick Martin provide depth. LB is adequately covered for 2026 barring another Greenlaw injury. Drops to tier-3 — still want long-term depth/succession since both starters are on one-year or injury-comeback timelines.

George Kittle tore his Achilles in January 2026 and will miss the early portion of the season. Tonges re-signing (2yr/$8M) helps bridge the gap, but he is not a Kittle-level starter. 49ers should still target a developmental TE in middle rounds of the draft. TE stays tier-2 for now — need is acute early in 2026 and they need long-term succession planning behind Kittle (age 33).

Lenoir and Green return as the top two CBs. Hobbs adds depth/competition on a low-risk 1yr deal. CB depth is adequate but not dominant — secondary has concerns at safety too. CB stays tier-3, likely addressed in mid-rounds of draft. New DC Raheem Morris expected to prioritize secondary improvement.

Ji'Ayir Brown and Malik Mustapha were both inconsistent in 2025 and are primarily in-the-box types. No safety was added in FA. With Raheem Morris as new DC emphasizing coverage versatility, an over-the-top free safety type is a clear draft/FA target. Safety escalates to tier-2 given the new scheme demands and existing roster limitations.

key_additions.headline: Future Hall of Famer ends 12-year Bucs tenure to give Purdy a true WR1 and elite red-zone target

key_additions.contract_summary: Trade (3yr remaining/$56.25M, $16.25M gtd in 2026); cost: 2026 3rd-round pick (No. 92)
key_additions.headline: Elite interior pass rusher (led DTs with 23 QB hits in 2025) addresses SF's worst 2025 weakness — a last-place NFL pass rush
Prodigal linebacker returns to SF on a prove-it deal to reunite with Fred Warner after injury-plagued year in Denver
NFL's most accurate kicker in 2025 (96.6% FG rate) locked up long-term before hitting market, ending years of 49ers kicker instability
Swing tackle depth amid Trent Williams contract uncertainty; provides insurance at LT if needed
Breakout backup TE (34 catches, 5 TDs filling in for Kittle in 2025) retained as Kittle recovers from torn Achilles
Packers cap casualty (previously $48M contract) adds depth and slot versatility to SF's secondary at minimal cost
Versatile interior backup (LG/C) adds depth after Aaron Banks departure; competes for starting LG role

key_losses.headline: Former star WR released after missing all of 2025 rehabbing knee and going AWOL from team; 49ers voided his guarantees
Team's 2025 lead receiver (55 catches, 9 TDs) expected to command $20M+/yr, pricing him out of San Francisco
Four-year starting LG departs to Green Bay, leaving a genuine starting vacancy at left guard
Team's leading sack producer (4 sacks) retires at 27, deepening an already-critical EDGE pass-rush need
Starting DT reunites with Robert Saleh in Tennessee; departure directly motivated Odighizuwa trade
Veteran WR departs as SF loses three receivers (Bourne, Jennings, Moore) in same FA cycle alongside Aiyuk release
Elite return specialist (27.5 yds/KR avg) departs, leaving SF without a proven returner

notes: Trent Williams contract situation remains unresolved as of 2026-03-13. Williams carries a $38.84M cap hit and the two sides have reportedly been at an impasse on a restructure. He has NOT been traded or released. Brandon Aiyuk release expected to be officially announced as a post-June 1 designation — confirmed by Lynch and Shanahan as decided but not yet officially processed per league timing. Mike Evans contract max value ($60.4M per Schefter) vs. base value ($42.4M per Spotrac/Meirov) discrepancy reflects heavy incentive structure — base value used in JSON as the contractually guaranteed structure. Jauan Jennings remains unsigned as of update date.
